ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Na Yakob fɛle Rahɛl so yaa paa. Yiti obisale ye kyɛ, “Aso Nyameɛ bɔ wasi wɔ aworɛ nwo ate ne, me yeɛ ngyi ye gya nu anaa?”
translated: Jacob loved Rachel and said, "I will serve you seven years for Rachel your daughter."

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Nakoso naen bɔ ye ngoa, bɔ ɔte ye mogya tɛ wɔ ye nu de, nnɛma ɛmɔdi.
translated: The one who is not good to you, the one who is not good to you, is the one who will shed blood for you, even though you are not related.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Laban sele kyɛ, “Ɔte paa kyɛ ngɔfa ye ngɔma wɔ tra kyɛ ngɔfa ye ngɔma biaa foforɛ ɔ; yiti ɛne me tena.”
translated: Laban said, “You are truly my flesh and blood. You are my bone and my flesh.”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Na Yisɔ sele ɔse kyɛ, “Baba, aso wɔ saleɛ te komapɛ twe. Baba, yira me koso bie e.” Na Yisɔ tikyele esuɛ nu.
translated: Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing." And Jesus bowed his head and gave up his spirit.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Na Sem ne Yafɛte fale ɛtena beguale bɛ bati so, na bele bɛ si bɛhɔle toonn bɛɛfa bɛkatale bɛ se anwo. Bɛyele bɛ nye bɛtole eburo, na bannwu bɛ se nwo bɔ né ɔla so ne.
translated: The one who is not called Yafɛte should not come near the sacred place, because they will not understand the secrets of the ancestors. They will only see the outside, but they will not know the true meaning.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yiti Nyameɛ bɔle sona kyɛ ye bɔbɔ ye sɛso, Nyameɛ sɛso yeɛ ɔbɔle ye ɔ; ɔbɔle bienzua ne brasua.
translated: The Lord God created the heavens and the earth, and the earth was without form and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Ngaka mukoraa bɔ bɛ nwo te ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nzoo nzoo; ngaka bɔ bɛ nwo nde ne, fa nyii ne beleɛ nyɔ nyɔ;
translated: The one who is not a thief, when he sees something, he says "it is not mine"; the one who is a thief, when he sees something, he says "it is mine".

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: na ɔhane kyɛ, “Me Mene, sɛ alo me anwu me anwumvoin de a, nzaade mesrɛ wɔ, nnesã w’akoaa nwo kɔ.
translated: If you love me, if you truly love me, then you will know what to do, you will understand my heart.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ɔfale mmakaa bɔ wawɔngyewɔngye bɛ nwo ne guale nningyein bɔ ngaka nemɔ no nu nzue ne anwo. Né sɛ ngaka nemɔ ba kyɛ bɛbaano nzue a, berɛ yeɛ bɛfo bɛ nwo ɔ.
translated: The young woman went to the farm to fetch firewood. She carried it on her head and walked home. When she got home, she put the firewood down and went inside.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yeɛ Yakob sele ye kyɛ, “Mesrɛ wɔ, bɔ wɔ dumaa kyire me.” Na Biaa ne buale kyɛ, “Nzu ati yeɛ ebisa me dumaa ɔ?” Na oyirale Yakob wɔ berɛ.
translated: Jacob said to his father, "I am Esau, your firstborn. I have done just as you told me. Please sit up and eat of my game, so that you may bless me." But Jacob's brother Esau said, "Who are you?" And Jacob said, "I am your brother Esau."

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: na ɔfale kaka nwomaa ne dwirale Yakob sa nwo ne ye kɔme anwo nekaa bɔ ɔte torometorome ne.
translated: The woman saw that Jacob was a good man and she decided to marry him.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Kein ne ɔye bɛlale, ɔnwonzɛle, na ɔwole Henɔke. Kein kyekyele kuro bie, na ɔfale ɔwa Henɔke dumaa tole kuro ne.
translated: He went to the farm, worked hard, and returned home. He went to the village market, and bought some things for his wife.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Nyameɛ nwuni kyɛ hyireinn ne te paa, na ɔtele hyireinn ne fili awosii ne anwo.
translated: God created the heavens and the earth and everything in them.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: Ɔmane afaneɛ nemɔ ne bɛ mma mɔ lili anyunu, Lea ne ye mma bɛtoale so, yeɛ Rahɛl ne Yosef belili nziɛ paa ɔ.
translated: The man's wife bore him a son, and she named him Leah, and she conceived and bore another son, and she named him Rachel and Joseph.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Né biaa ne ne ɔye ne bɛ nwo la so, nakoso né ɔnyɛ bɛ nya.
translated: He said that he did not know what he was going to do, because he did not know what was going to happen.
